title: Supabase menu_items table — the BroMenu seed dataset (40+ rows: Chick-fil-A, McDonald's, Starbucks, Subway)
html:
TL;DR

A Supabase table editor screenshot of the BroMenu / Brotein menu_items table — schema columns visible: id (uuid), restaurant_id (uuid), name (text), description (text). ~40 rows visible spanning Chick-fil-A, McDonald's, Starbucks, Subway, and others.

The restaurant_id values cluster into a tiny enum-like set — 6e9b7a10-0001-0001-0001-000000000001 through ...000003 plus ...000025 — which means restaurants are seed-IDed with low-numbered UUIDs (a common DX pattern when you want predictable IDs for fixtures).

Why it's in the graph

This screenshot is the architectural ground truth for everything in the July 17–18 nutrition-PDF screenshot burst (Chipotle, Little Caesars, Wingstop, Taco Bell ×3). Captured July 9, 8 days before the nutrition scrapes started — Yahir built the schema first, then went hunting for data to pour in. Classic build-the-pipe-then-fill-it move.

The visible item names also leak product strategy: he's seeded with breakfast-heavy / protein-heavy items first (Egg White & Roasted Red Pepper Egg Bites, Bacon & Gruyère Egg Bites, Cheese & Fruit Protein Box, PB&J Protein Box, Eggs & Cheddar Protein Box, Spinach Feta & Egg White Wrap, Egg McMuffin, Sausage McMuffin w/ Egg, Bacon Egg & Cheese McGriddles, Steak Egg & Cheese Bagel, Impossible Breakfast Sandwich, Carved Turkey, Roast Beef, Tuna). The seed dataset isn't "everything Chick-fil-A sells" — it's "things a anabolic scoring app would surface as good picks." So the seeding strategy was top-down by anabolic merit, not bottom-up by restaurant catalog.

What's actually on the page

  • Schema: id uuid (random v4), restaurant_id uuid (seed-numbered), name text, description text (mostly NULL).
  • Visible items (sample, deduped):
    • Chick fil A: Spicy Deluxe Sandwich, Spicy Chicken Sandwich, Chick-fil-A Chicken Sandwich, Market Salad, Grilled Nuggets
    • McDonald's: Sausage McMuffin, Sausage Biscuit (with/without Egg), Sausage McMuffin with Egg, Sausage McGriddles, Bacon Egg & Cheese McGriddles, Egg McMuffin, Deluxe McCrispy, Spicy McCrispy, McChicken Deluxe, Fruit & Maple Oatmeal, Impossible Breakfast Sandwich
    • Starbucks: Egg White & Roasted Red Pepper Egg Bites, Bacon & Gruyère Egg Bites, Cheese & Fruit Protein Box, PB&J Protein Box, Eggs & Cheddar Protein Box, Perfect Bar Peanut Butter, Spinach Feta & Egg White Wrap, Plain Bagel, Everything Bagel, Bagel (plain), Gluten-Free Marshmallow Dream Bar
    • Subway: Steak & Cheese, Chicken Strips, Carved Turkey, Tuna, Roast Beef
    • One unidentified: "Double-Smoked Bacon, Cheddar & Egg Sandwic[h]" (likely Starbucks)
    • "Sausage, Egg & Cheese Bagel" (Starbucks or generic)
  • Restaurant ID enum-like values: ...000001, ...000002, ...000003, ...000025 — gaps suggest more chains exist that just don't have items in this view yet.

Loose ends

  • The schema is thin — no calories, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, serving_size columns visible. Either those live in a join table (menu_item_nutrition?) or this is pre-nutrition-data. Given the July 17–18 OCR scrape sprint, possibly the latter — the table was still bootstrapped on names only.
  • Worth a densify pass once a bromenu data model page exists.
  • The fact that 45+ items are already loaded by July 9 means there's a script somewhere for this — Apple Notes? Cursor session? Web Clipper? Not yet ingested.

Links

  • supabase
  • bromenu / brotein / anabolic scoring
  • chick fil a / mcdonalds / starbucks / subway
  • fast food nutrition dataset
  • restaurant menu scraping
  • bromenu data model
